20001.question_text:
Q.1 [11916616] 
												ABC is a triangle having area of 350 cm2. D is a
												point on side BC, such that AD is the angle bisector of ∠CAB. If AB : AC
												= 3 : 4, then what is the area (in cm2) of triangle ADC?

20002.question_text:
Q.2 [11916616] 
											Pipes P and Q are inlet pipes while R is an outlet pipe. Pipe P
											supplies water at the rate of 30 liters
											per hour, pipe Q fills the tank in 6 hours while R empties it in 24 hours.
											The empty tank gets filled
											in 2 hours when all the three pipes are opened. Find the capacity of the
											tank.

20003.question_text:
Q.3 [11916616]  Two
									concentric circles, having their center at O, are cut by a line such that the line
									forms a chord
									for both the circles. The length of the chord for the smaller circle and the bigger
									circle is 8m and
									16m respectively. What is the absolute difference between the squares of the radii
									(in cm2) of the
									two circles?

20004.question_text:
Q.4 [11916616]  In how many
								ways can four couples be seated around a circular table so that the same gender
								does not get to sit together and exactly one of the four couples sit adjacent to each
								other?

20005.question_text:
Q.5 [11916616]  The average age of
							a family of 5 members is 20 years. If the age of the youngest member is 10
							years, what was the approximate average age of the family just a day before the birth of the
							youngest member?

20006.question_text:
Q.6 [11916616]  There are 3 vessels of
						equal volume, filled with mixtures of water and milk in the ratio of 1 : 2, 2 : 3
						and 3 : 4 respectively. They all are poured in a large vessel. What proportion of the liquid
						must
						be removed from that vessel and replaced with water of equal quantity so that the resulting
						mixture in the vessel contains 50% milk?

20007.question_text:
Q.7 [11916616]  Find the sum of the
					following series upto 10 terms:

20008.question_text:
Q.8 [11916616]  The number of votes not cast
				for the PNC Party increased by 25% in the National General Election
				(NGE) as compared to the previous Assembly Polls, and as a result the PNC Party lost in the NGE
				by a majority of twice as many votes by which it had won the Assembly Polls. If the total of
				2,60,000 people voted each time, then how many of them voted for the PNC Party in the previous
				Assembly Polls?

20009.question_text:
Q.9 [11916616]  Find the number of common
				roots of the following cubic equations:
				7x3 + 9x2 + 11x + 13 = 0 and 7x3 + 8x2 + 16x + 7 = 0

20010.question_text:
Q.10 [11916616]  If f(x + y, x – y) = x × y,
				then find the expression for f(x, y).

20011.question_text:
Q.11 [11916616]  The monthly telephone bills
				of a company has a fixed tariff of Rs. 250 for up to 50 outgoing calls, above
				which charge of Rs. 1.25 per call needs to be paid. If the ratio of the monthly bills paid by A and B
				is 2 : 3 and the number of outgoing calls by A is 90, then what is the total number of outgoing calls
				made by B in that month?

20012.question_text:
Q.12 [11916616]  How many 3-digit numbers
				leave a remainder of 4 when divided by 21 and a remainder of 8 when
				divided by 13?

20013.question_text:
Q.13 [11916616]  Two cars A and B start
				simultaneously from two points P and Q respectively, located at a distance
				of 200 metres and 800 metres respectively from the point of intersection of two perpendicular roads
				L1 and L2. Car A is moving along the road L1 and is going away from the
				point of intersection of the
				two roads while car B is moving along the road L2 and is moving towards the point of
				intersection of the
				roads. If after 2 minutes and again after 6 minutes from the start, the two cars are at equal distance
				from the
				point of intersection of the roads, then find the ratio of the speeds of the two cars.

20015.question_text:
Q.15 [11916616]  If |x + 1| + |x| + |x – 1| +
				|x – 1| + |x – 2| + |x – 3| ≤ 30, then for how many integral values of x does this hold true?

20016.question_text:
Q.16 [11916616]  A trader buys goods at a 19%
				discount on the label price. If he wants to make a profit of 20% after
				allowing a discount of 10%, then by what percentage should his marked price be greater than the original
				label
				price?

20017.question_text:
Q.17 [11916616]  If P = x2 +
				5y2 – 4xy + 6y + 29 where x and y are integers such that –8 ≤ y ≤ 1 and –3 ≤ x ≤ 2, then
				which of the
				following is always true?
options: P can never be more than 270
P can never be more than 301
P can never be less than 29
P can never be less than 22

20018.question_text:
Q.18 [11916616]  A sum of money becomes 8
				times in 27 years at r% compound interest. Had the same amount was
				invested at r% simple interest for 27 years, then how many times would the amount have become after 27
				years?
options: greater than 3 times but less than 4 times
greater than 2 times but less than 3 times

20019.question_text:
Q.19 [11916616]  How many integral values of
				(x, y) satisfy the equation x2 – y2 = 220?

20020.question_text:
Q.20 [11916616]  If A = {91, 92, 93, …, 180}
				and B is a subset of A such that the sum of no two elements of B is a multiple of 9, then what can be
				the maximum number of elements
				in B?

20021.question_text:
Q.21 [11916616]  In the figure given below, a
				circle with the centre at O and a rectangle ABCD are drawn. The circle
				touches side CD at E and passes through the points A and F. If AO = 3 cm, DF = 1 cm and DC =
				8 cm, then the area (in cm2) of rectangle ABCD is

20022.question_text:
Q.22 [11916616]  A and B start running
				simultaneously towards each other from the two ends of a track XY and the ratio of their speeds is 3 :
				4. Every time they meet, they interchange their speeds and also reverse
				their directions. At how many distinct points on the track do they meet each other, if they run
				continuously between 2 ends of the tracks? (Track XY is shown below)
